transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Kendra.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, I'm Dennis. I'm calling because I've relapsed. I did a 30-day residential about two years ago and I was clean for fourteen months. I started drinking again four months ago and it's gotten bad fast.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Dennis, thank you for being so upfront about your history. Fourteen months of sobriety after residential — that's real work. And the fact that you're calling now, rather than waiting for things to get worse, tells me you haven't forgotten what it felt like to be on the other side of this. What's been going on in the last four months?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Stressful job change. And a breakup. I know those are excuses, but that's when it started.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: They're not excuses — they're context, and our clinical team needs that context to design the right plan for you. You've done residential before, so you know the drill. Can I ask: how much are you drinking now versus before you got into treatment the first time?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: About the same. Daily, a bottle of wine to a bottle and a half.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And are you having any withdrawal symptoms when you try to go a day without?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Some. Anxiety, trouble sleeping. Nothing as bad as last time, but it's there.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good to know. That history matters — your body remembers. I want to flag that for our clinical team. Dennis, given that you've been through residential, what's your thinking on level of care this time? Some people want to try IOP first, others know they need the structure of residential again.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Honestly I think I need residential. I don't trust myself in an outpatient setting right now.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That kind of self-awareness is valuable and I'll make sure it goes into your intake notes. Our clinical team will assess that formally, but your own read matters. Let me get your insurance information so we can check what your plan covers for a residential admission.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure. It's Meridian Care, member ID BP93660796, group GRP-444091.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. And from your first treatment — do you remember if there were any complications with coverage, or did it go smoothly?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: It went fine. Same carrier, different plan.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good. That continuity actually helps. I'm noting your prior treatment facility, your length of sobriety, and the relapse timeline. Our intake team will have all of this before they call you. Here's what happens next: benefits verification will be back to you within ninety minutes, and our admissions coordinator will call immediately after with availability for an assessment. Can you take a call this afternoon?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect — you'll hear from us between one and two o'clock. Dennis, relapse is not failure. It's information. You know how to do this.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you. I needed to hear that.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Strong handling of relapse caller — empathy was warm but appropriately matter-of-fact (not over-pitying), clinical history covered thoroughly including prior treatment complications. Agent explicitly asked caller's own level-of-care preference, which is excellent practice. Minor: empathy opening was good but could have slowed down slightly before moving to clinical questions.
